priority_levels.P0_immediate: Preserve or create within 24 hours because loss, signing, deletion, or timeline risk is high.
priority_levels.P1_core: Core proof for classification, claim basis, or amount.
priority_levels.P2_supporting: Supporting proof that improves credibility, continuity, or negotiation leverage.
priority_levels.P3_local_verify: Local rule, government record, or professional verification item.

global_safety_rules: Do not fabricate, alter, backdate, or coach false evidence.
Do not bypass account permissions or copy company secrets, source code, customer lists, or unrelated personal data.
For employer-controlled materials, request in writing or ask the arbitration tribunal to order production.
Preserve screenshots, exports, and timelines only from materials the worker can lawfully access.
Separate facts, assumptions, and legal conclusions.

items.proves: labor relationship, contract term, position, workplace, wage terms, renewal history
items.lawful_source: worker copy, onboarding files, email, company HR system if lawfully accessible
items.collection_note: Preserve full copies and renewal versions; do not backdate missing contracts.

items.proves: wage level, payment continuity, employer identity, social insurance payment status
items.lawful_source: bank records, tax app, social insurance system, housing fund system, payslips
items.collection_note: Export or screenshot monthly records with timestamps where possible.

items.proves: actual work, last working day, access removal, attendance disputes
items.lawful_source: attendance app, badge records, work email, lawful screenshots
items.collection_note: Preserve only records related to the worker and dispute timeline.

items.proves: consistent timeline of proposal, notice, objection, handover, lockout, and payment events
items.lawful_source: worker-created factual memo based on existing records
items.collection_note: Write dates, participants, exact words, documents, and open questions; do not add facts not remembered or recorded.

items.proves: employer statements, termination reason, resignation pressure, settlement terms, notice timing
items.lawful_source: worker's chats, emails, meeting invitations, lawful notes
items.collection_note: Preserve full conversation context, not isolated fragments.

items.proves: worker's disagreement, request for written reason, request for continued work or documents
items.lawful_source: truthful email, message, or letter sent by the worker
items.collection_note: Keep wording factual and calm; request written basis, payment details, and document copies.

items.proves: average monthly wage, bonus, allowance, subsidy, commission, overtime, deductions
items.lawful_source: bank statements, payslips, tax records, payroll emails, bonus documents
items.collection_note: Preserve the 12 months before termination or the actual shorter employment period.

items.proves: employment start date, continuous service, transfer between entities, renewal history
items.lawful_source: contract, offer, onboarding records, social insurance, tax, emails
items.collection_note: Flag affiliated-company transfers and outsourced/labor-dispatch facts for lawyer_check.

items.proves: local high-wage cap and calculation baseline
items.lawful_source: official local statistics or human resources/social security bureau data
items.collection_note: Use current city and termination-year data; do not generalize another city's cap.

items.proves: agreement terms, release scope, payment date, waiver language, employer-proposed termination
items.lawful_source: drafts, signed copy, HR email, document system if lawfully accessible
items.collection_note: Preserve every version before signing; check all-disputes-settled language.

items.proves: whether employer proposed mutual termination, affecting economic compensation path
items.lawful_source: HR chat, email, meeting notes, calendar invite
items.collection_note: Do not infer employer proposal without written or contextual proof.

items.proves: resignation reason, timing, recipient, voluntary or statutory basis
items.lawful_source: resignation letter, email, OA submission, delivery proof
items.collection_note: If not yet sent, wording must truthfully reflect actual statutory employer breaches.

items.proves: unpaid wages, unpaid social insurance, unsafe conditions, coercion, invalid rules, or other statutory breach
items.lawful_source: payroll, bank, tax, social insurance records, messages, notices
items.collection_note: Match the resignation reason to actual evidence; do not rewrite history.

items.proves: employer's stated fault ground and termination date
items.lawful_source: termination notice, email, HR message, termination certificate
items.collection_note: Ask for written reason if employer only states it orally.

items.proves: validity and publication of rules used for serious violation
items.lawful_source: employee handbook, rule acknowledgment, union/employee congress records
items.collection_note: Request lawfully; employer often controls democratic procedure records.

items.proves: facts of misconduct, damage, consistency, proportionality, and proof burden
items.lawful_source: investigation records, warning records, performance data, comparable cases
items.collection_note: Do not take unrelated confidential files; request production where needed.

items.proves: the exact employer-stated non-fault reason and whether the case is framed as art40 instead of economic layoff
items.lawful_source: termination notice, optimization notice, HR email, meeting minutes
items.collection_note: Ask for written reason if employer only states AI transformation, role mismatch, or objective change orally.

items.proves: illness, incompetence after training/transfer, or major objective change basis
items.lawful_source: written notice, performance records, medical restrictions, objective-change documents
items.collection_note: Separate the asserted ground from ordinary business preference.

items.proves: training, position adjustment, reassessment, or failed contract-change consultation
items.lawful_source: training records, transfer notice, meeting minutes, emails
items.collection_note: Ask for missing employer-controlled documents in writing.

items.proves: 30-day notice or one-month substitute notice wage
items.lawful_source: notice date, payroll, settlement sheet, bank record
items.collection_note: Use previous month's wage for substitute notice wage verification.

items.proves: the employer-stated economic layoff reason, implementation date, and whether the case is framed as economic layoff rather than art40 non-fault dismissal
items.lawful_source: termination notice, layoff notice, optimization notice, HR email, meeting minutes, termination certificate
items.collection_note: Ask for written reason if employer only states business adjustment, AI transformation, redundancy, or optimization orally.

items.proves: number or percentage of workers laid off and statutory layoff reason
items.lawful_source: layoff plan, announcement, workforce count, public filings where available
items.collection_note: Preserve group notices lawfully; do not collect unrelated personnel files.

items.proves: AI transformation or major technology/business-method adjustment, proposed contract changes, internal alternatives, and why layoffs remained necessary
items.lawful_source: transformation plan, revised organization chart, new role descriptions, consultation records, internal transfer or upskilling notices
items.collection_note: For art41, test the contract-change and internal-alternative chain; do not treat ordinary onboarding training as universally mandatory unless the employer relies on incompetence under art40.

items.proves: 30-day explanation to union/all employees, opinion collection, labor authority report
items.lawful_source: union notice, employee meeting notice, opinion records, labor authority filing proof
items.collection_note: If unavailable, list as employer-controlled and request production.

items.proves: Guangzhou written HRSS report package before implementation, including layoff reason, affected worker basic information, union or all-staff explanation and opinion record, responsible person contact, final layoff plan, supporting materials, HRSS feedback response, and final publication to all staff
items.lawful_source: employer written report, layoff plan, opinion records, HRSS submission proof, HRSS opinion or suggestion response, final plan announcement
items.collection_note: Request production in writing or through arbitration; do not copy unrelated personnel, financial, customer, source-code, or strategy files.

items.proves: priority retention status and six-month rehire priority
items.lawful_source: contract term, family burden proof, recruitment posts, rehire notices
items.collection_note: Track new recruitment within six months after layoff; preserve public job posts and employer notices with timestamps.

items.proves: whether the employer recruits again, uses substantially similar outsourced or dispatched labor, or replaces the laid-off role within six months after an economic layoff
items.lawful_source: official recruitment pages, public job boards, recruiter messages received by the worker, lawful screenshots of same-role openings, rehire notices, outsourcing or dispatch facts known from public or worker-accessible channels
items.collection_note: Collect only public or lawfully accessible materials; do not scrape private accounts, impersonate candidates, copy internal staffing lists, or take unrelated commercial secrets.

items.proves: expiry date, renewal offers, refusal reason, two fixed-term renewals, open-ended contract trigger
items.lawful_source: contracts, renewal emails, OA workflow, HR notices
items.collection_note: Preserve all contract versions and renewal communications.

items.proves: continued employment after expiry and employer acquiescence
items.lawful_source: attendance, payroll, task assignment, work email, access logs
items.collection_note: Track work after expiry by date and task.

items.proves: unilateral salary reduction, job transfer, changed duties/workplace, worker objection
items.lawful_source: transfer notice, salary notice, contract terms, written objection
items.collection_note: Object truthfully and promptly; keep attendance strategy lawful.

items.proves: unpaid wages, late payment, social insurance non-payment or gap months
items.lawful_source: bank, tax, payroll, social insurance records, housing fund records
items.collection_note: Verify local treatment of social insurance underpayment, late payment, and non-payment.

items.proves: employer-side exclusion from work disguised as resignation or pressure
items.lawful_source: account lockout screenshots, access denial, forced leave notice, HR chats
items.collection_note: Preserve timestamps and full context; do not bypass access controls.

items.proves: pregnancy, maternity, nursing, medical-period, work-injury, or occupational-disease status relevant to protected-status or wage-difference claims
items.lawful_source: medical certificate, maternity record, statutory notice, work injury or occupational disease documents held by the worker
items.collection_note: Use the minimum necessary proof and redact unrelated diagnosis, family, address, ID, phone, and third-party information before sharing outside formal channels.

items.proves: conflict among resignation, termination notice, agreement, certificate, and HR statements
items.lawful_source: all notices, agreements, resignation forms, certificates, HR chats
items.collection_note: Keep conflicting documents together; do not let one label control classification.

items.proves: worker requested employer's basis, date, and settlement calculation
items.lawful_source: worker email, letter, message to HR or manager
items.collection_note: Ask for written reason before signing new characterization.

items.proves: arbitration limitation, wage-related special timing, urgency
items.lawful_source: worker-created timeline plus termination and wage dates
items.collection_note: Escalate to lawyer_check if limitation may be close.
